team_context.cap_situation: tight — ~$13M in effective cap space after cuts/signings, heavy spending on defense (Burns, Lawrence, Adebo, Holland), new HC John Harbaugh installed with 5-year deal. Restructures of Burns/Adebo/Holland possible to create more room.
team_context.trade_posture: Building around Jaxson Dart and young core. New HC Harbaugh brings win-now urgency but roster is 4-13 caliber. Would move overpaid veterans or redundant pieces for picks, but core young players (Dart, Nabers, Nubin, Phillips) are locked in. Willing to acquire proven starters at positions of need (WR, IOL, DL depth).

players.name: Jaxson Dart
players.performance_evidence: Named NFL All-Rookie Team QB by Dane Brugler. Accounted for 24 TDs (most among rookie QBs), 2,272 passing yards in limited starts. Won NFL Offensive Rookie of the Month in October. First QB in history with passing + rushing TD in 4 consecutive games. Flashed franchise-caliber upside but on a 4-13 team.

trade_value.reasoning: Young franchise QB on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years + 5th-year option of control. His trade value is astronomical because QB is the most valuable position and he showed legit franchise potential as a rookie. Comparable to early Jayden Daniels/Caleb Williams value. Giants would never trade him but theoretical value exceeds multiple 1sts.

players.availability_reasoning: The entire Giants rebuild is structured around Dart. Harbaugh was hired partly based on Dart's potential. Every offseason move (Likely, Eluemunor, Edmunds) is designed to support him. Zero chance of trade.
players.injury_notes: Required 5 concussion evaluations in 2025 rookie season, missed time with one diagnosed concussion. Aggressive running style raised concerns. Must be monitored going forward.
players.trade_buzz: There was brief chatter about resetting the QB clock with a top-2 pick in 2026 draft, but that door closed when Giants got pick #5 instead.

players.name: Jameis Winston
players.performance_evidence: Made one start in 2025 (Week 11 vs Packers) when Dart was in concussion protocol. Veteran presence signed to mentor Dart behind Russell Wilson. Limited production in spot duty.

trade_value.reasoning: Veteran backup QB on an expiring deal. Minimal trade value — teams can sign comparable QBs in free agency. Only worth a conditional late-round pick to a team with a sudden QB injury.

players.availability_reasoning: Veteran backup entering final year. If a team needs a bridge QB due to injury, Giants would part with him for any draft compensation. His value is primarily as Dart's mentor.

players.name: Cam Skattebo
players.performance_evidence: Ranked 3rd among all RBs by PFF before injury. Was a revelation — 398 rushing yards, 5 TDs, 189 receiving yards in limited action before dislocated ankle in Week 8 ended his season. Fan favorite, physical runner compared to Mike Alstott. 5th in Heisman voting in college.

trade_value.reasoning: Exciting young RB on a 4th-round rookie deal with 3 years of control. RB position depresses trade value, but his low cost and dual-threat ability push him to early-3rd range. Coming off serious ankle injury adds risk.

players.availability_reasoning: Fan favorite, integral to Harbaugh's physical run-game vision. Giants built their 2025 offense around the Dart-Skattebo connection. New OC Matt Nagy will feature 12-personnel with Skattebo as the lead back. No chance of trade.
players.injury_notes: Dislocated right ankle in Week 8 of 2025, required season-ending surgery. GM Schoen said he's trending toward being ready for training camp. Must be monitored.

players.name: Tyrone Tracy Jr.
players.performance_evidence: Led Giants RBs with 740 rushing yards and 1,123 total yards from scrimmage in 2024 rookie season. Solid but unspectacular 2025 — dislocated shoulder in Week 3 limited his play. Explosive but inconsistent.

trade_value.reasoning: Cheap, young complementary RB with 2 years left. RB position value caps his ceiling. Worth a late-round pick to a team needing depth. Skattebo's emergence as RB1 makes Tracy somewhat expendable.

players.availability_reasoning: With Skattebo entrenched as RB1 and Devin Singletary still on the roster (restructured deal), Tracy is a quality backup but not irreplaceable. Giants would listen on offers of a 5th-round pick or better.
players.injury_notes: Dislocated shoulder in Week 3 of 2025 season. Has dealt with various minor injuries.

players.name: Malik Nabers
players.performance_evidence: Set multiple Giants rookie records in 2024: 109 receptions (franchise record), 1,000+ yards. Was on pace for monster sophomore year before torn ACL in Week 4 of 2025 (18 rec, 271 yards in 4 games including 9/167/2 TD vs Dallas). 5th-year option available in 2028. Generational WR talent.

trade_value.reasoning: 22-year-old potential All-Pro WR on a cheap rookie deal with 5th-year option. Even with the ACL, his age, talent, and contract make him one of the most valuable non-QB trade assets in the NFL. Extension-eligible after 2026 and projected to command $150M+ when the time comes.

players.availability_reasoning: Cornerstone of the franchise. Set to be Dart's top weapon for years. Giants will exercise the 5th-year option and eventually extend him. The dead cap alone ($17.3M) makes a trade impossible even if they wanted to, which they absolutely do not.
players.injury_notes: Torn right ACL in Week 4 of 2025 season. GM Schoen says he's trending to be ready for training camp 2026. ACL recovery adds uncertainty for a player whose game relies on cutting and acceleration.

players.name: Darius Slayton
players.performance_evidence: Disappointing 2025 — beat reporters flagged concerns about his production. Had a solid 2024 but regressed. Coming off a $16M cap hit which is overpaid for his current production level. Giants are 'stuck with' his contract per reporting.

trade_value.reasoning: Massively overpaid relative to production. $16M cap hit with $15.75M dead cap makes him virtually untradeable — any acquiring team would need to take on the full cap hit, and no team will pay $16M for a declining WR2. Negative trade value when factoring in the contract.

players.availability_reasoning: Giants would love to move Slayton's contract but the dead cap ($15.75M) means cutting or trading him saves almost nothing. They're essentially stuck paying him in 2026. Could become a post-June 1 cut candidate. Beat reporters suggest he's not a fit in the new offense.
players.trade_buzz: Dan Duggan of The Athletic noted Slayton is 'returning on a $16M cap hit despite a concerning 2025 performance.' Giants can't easily move on.

players.name: Beaux Collins
players.performance_evidence: UDFA who made the 53-man roster as the only undrafted player to do so in 2025. Former Clemson/Notre Dame WR with size and physicality but inconsistent hands (20 drops in college). Depth receiver who benefits from Wan'Dale Robinson's departure.

trade_value.reasoning: Minimum-salary depth WR with no meaningful trade value. Any team could sign a comparable player off the street.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth piece with minimal investment. Giants would trade him for anything of value but nobody is calling for a WR4 on a minimum deal.

players.name: Isaiah Likely
players.performance_evidence: 135 career receptions for 1,568 yards and 15 TDs in Baltimore. Career-high 42 catches, 477 yards, 6 TDs in 2024. Declined in 2025 (27/307/1) behind Mark Andrews with injuries. 3rd-highest paid TE in NFL. Athletic 6-4, 245 lb frame with 70% catch rate and 11.6 YPR career.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old TE on a new 3-year deal paid as a top-3 TE. Excellent athletic profile but unproven as a true #1 option. High dead cap ($28M) on a brand-new contract makes a trade impossible in year 1. Value is theoretical.

players.availability_reasoning: Literally just signed. Harbaugh hand-picked him from Baltimore. Dead cap of $28M makes any trade financially impossible. Central to Giants' 12-personnel offensive identity.
players.injury_notes: Missed first 3 games of 2025 with a foot fracture. Had fumbling issues late in 2025 season.

players.name: Theo Johnson
players.performance_evidence: 29 receptions for 331 yards and 1 TD as rookie in 2024. Dart connected with him for 2 TDs in Week 5 vs Saints in 2025. Athletic 6-6, 259 lb frame from Penn State. Still developing as a blocker. Will share TE duties with Likely in 12-personnel looks.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, athletic TE2 on a cheap deal. Some upside but hasn't proven himself as a starter. Worth a late-round pick to a TE-needy team but the Giants need him for their 12-personnel base.

players.availability_reasoning: Giants plan to use 12-personnel extensively under Nagy/Harbaugh, making both TEs important. Would need a solid offer (4th-round pick) to part with him given their scheme needs.

players.name: Andrew Thomas
players.performance_evidence: 2nd-team All-Pro in 2022, earning his 5-year $117.5M extension. Has been inconsistent since due to injuries — Lisfranc injury ended 2024 season in Week 6, limited snap count to start 2025. When healthy, he's a top-10 LT. Anchors the left side protecting Dart's blind side.

trade_value.reasoning: Pro Bowl-caliber LT when healthy, 27 years old with 3 years remaining. Massive dead cap ($36.1M) makes a trade financially impossible. Even if it were possible, his talent + age + contract years would command a 1st-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Dead cap of $36.1M makes any trade impossible. He's the Giants' franchise LT protecting their franchise QB. Contract was restructured in 2025, further tying him to the team.
players.injury_notes: Lisfranc injury ended 2024 season (Week 6). Limited at start of 2025. History of foot/ankle issues is a real concern. Joe Schoen has been 'conservative about restructuring players with injury histories' per Dan Duggan.

players.name: Jon Runyan
players.performance_evidence: 52.9 PFF grade in 2025, ranked 67th among all guards. Signed 3-year $30M deal in 2024 but has underperformed expectations. Described as 'closer to adequate' than good by former team beat writer. Below-average starter who is overpaid.

trade_value.reasoning: Overpaid guard on an expiring deal with no guaranteed money. $11.75M cap hit for below-average play means negative trade value. Would have to include a pick sweetener to move him. More likely a cut candidate.

players.availability_reasoning: Multiple beat reporters have identified him as a top cut candidate. $0 guaranteed remaining, $9.25M in cap savings if released with only $2.5M dead cap. Giants nearly missed retaining Eluemunor due to cap constraints — Runyan is the easiest cap relief available. May accept a restructured deal to stay.
players.trade_buzz: Dan Duggan and multiple outlets have flagged Runyan as the Giants' most likely cap casualty. SI suggested he'd need to take a pay cut to remain on the roster.

players.name: John Michael Schmitz Jr.
players.performance_evidence: 26.9 PFF pass blocking grade as a rookie (worst among centers). Improved in 2024 but still below average. Dealt with injuries. 'Poor play and injury woes' noted by beat reporters heading into 2026. Still the starter by default given lack of alternatives.

trade_value.reasoning: Below-average starting center entering final year of rookie deal. Cheap ($4.2M cap hit) but limited production caps value. A team desperate for interior OL depth might offer a 6th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Entering his contract year with 'doubts about the future of the center position' per beat reporting. Giants missed on Tyler Linderbaum in free agency, suggesting they're open to upgrading. Could draft a center or acquire one, making Schmitz available.
players.injury_notes: Placed on IR late in 2025 season. Has dealt with injuries throughout career.

players.name: Jake Kubas
players.performance_evidence: UDFA from North Dakota State who played 5 games with 3 starts in 2024 before being waived and re-signed to practice squad in 2025. Cross-trained at center. Depth piece who showed promise but was not active for games in 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: Practice squad-level IOL with minimal NFL experience. Essentially no trade value.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth interior lineman on a minimum deal. Giants would move him for anything of value but there's no market for practice squad guards.

players.name: Jermaine Eluemunor
players.performance_evidence: Career resurgence with Giants — started 31 games over 2024-25, played 1,000+ snaps for first time in 2025. 88.8% pass-block win rate (2nd-highest of career). Allowed only 4 sacks in 2025. ESPN gave re-signing a B grade. Dart personally recruited him to stay.

trade_value.reasoning: Above-average RT on a fair 3-year deal at $13M/yr. Solid but not elite. Dead cap of $22M on a brand-new contract makes a trade financially impossible in 2026. Age (31) limits long-term value.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed on a 3-year, $39M deal. Harbaugh named him a top priority. Dart personally lobbied for his return. Dead cap makes trade impossible. Anchors RT position protecting Dart.

players.name: Darius Alexander
players.performance_evidence: 3rd-round pick in 2025 out of Toledo. 6-4, 305 lbs. Drafted to compete alongside Dexter Lawrence on the interior DL. Appeared on Monday Night Raw with teammates in staged WWE segment. Limited rookie production — was a developmental piece in 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, cheap DL on a rookie deal with 3 years left. Hasn't proven much but the investment and upside make him a hold. Worth a late-round pick based on draft pedigree alone.

players.availability_reasoning: Young, cheap, and developing. Giants won't give him away but haven't seen enough to make him untouchable. Would need at least a 4th-round pick to consider moving him.

players.name: Dexter Lawrence II
players.performance_evidence: 3 consecutive Pro Bowl selections, 2 All-Pro 2nd-team honors. 9 sacks in 2024 (12 games) before elbow injury. Massive decline in 2025: only 31 tackles, 0.5 sacks, 8 QB hits. Was the 'most dominant defensive player in football' before Thanksgiving 2024 elbow injury per reporting. Still among most double-teamed players in league.

trade_value.reasoning: Former All-Pro DT entering age-28 season. Massive $27M cap hit hurts, but trading him saves $13M with $13.9M dead cap — making it financially viable. One source valued him at 'a second-round pick and a mid-rounder.' If he bounces back to 2024 form, he's a top-5 DT.

players.availability_reasoning: SNY's Connor Hughes reports a trade 'can't be ruled out.' One front office source valued him at a 2nd + mid-rounder. The $13M in cap savings is enticing for a cap-strapped team. However, Harbaugh's plan is to build an aggressive defensive front around Lawrence. Dan Duggan suggests an extension is more likely. A trade would require an overpay.
players.injury_notes: Elbow injury before Thanksgiving 2024 preceded a massive production decline. 2025 was the worst season of his career. The cause of his regression remains unclear.
players.trade_buzz: Connor Hughes (SNY): trade 'can't be ruled out.' Bleacher Report suggested a package from the Bengals yielding a 2026 2nd. Dan Duggan (The Athletic) proposed a 2-year, $56M extension as more likely path.

players.name: Roy Robertson-Harris
players.performance_evidence: Veteran DL signed as a free agent in 2025. Provides rotational depth next to Lawrence. Not a high-impact player but a scheme fit as a gap-eating run defender. Experienced veteran presence.

trade_value.reasoning: 33-year-old rotational DL on a 1-year deal. Minimal trade value — any interested team could sign comparable players in free agency.

players.availability_reasoning: Veteran depth piece with no dead cap. If the Giants need cap room during the season or a team loses a DT to injury, he could be moved for a conditional late-round pick.

players.name: Tremaine Edmunds
players.performance_evidence: 112 tackles, 4 INTs (career high), 9 PD, 1 sack in 2025 with Bears. Ranked 6th-best off-ball LB by PFSN with 89.3 Impact Score. 81.3 PFF coverage grade. Has 100+ tackles in every season played. 2x Pro Bowler (2019, 2020). 900 career tackles, 14 career INTs.

trade_value.reasoning: Just signed 3-year, $36M deal. High dead cap ($20M) makes 2026 trade impossible. Solid veteran LB who fills a clear need. 28 years old with some upside in a new scheme under Dennard Wilson.

players.availability_reasoning: Brand-new signing with $23.7M guaranteed. Giants released Okereke specifically to sign Edmunds. Dead cap makes trade impossible. Central to Dennard Wilson's new defensive scheme.
players.injury_notes: Got banged up and sputtered down the stretch of 2025 per ESPN sources, but multiple sources believe he can still play at a high level.

players.name: Micah McFadden
players.performance_evidence: Re-signed on 1-year, $3.75M prove-it deal. Played in 48 games with 36 starts over 4 seasons. Elite run-gap shooter with high motor. Missed most of 2025 with Lisfranc injury (suffered Week 1). Began on-field running drills in December and is expected to compete for starting job alongside Edmunds.

trade_value.reasoning: Solid but unspectacular ILB on a cheap 1-year deal coming off a Lisfranc injury. Worth a conditional late-round pick to a LB-needy team.

players.availability_reasoning: 1-year prove-it deal. If the Giants draft a LB at #5 (Sonny Styles has been mentioned) or early in the draft, McFadden becomes expendable. Would move him for any reasonable draft compensation.
players.injury_notes: Lisfranc surgery in September 2025. Reports are overwhelmingly positive on recovery — began running drills in December.

players.name: Paulson Adebo
players.performance_evidence: 71 tackles, 5 PD, 1 INT in 2025. Allowed 66.2% catch rate (49/74), 532 yards, 2 TDs. Committed 3 penalties. 'Not Patrick Surtain II, but a quality starting cornerback' per Big Blue View. 'Didn't look like a $54M answer in Year 1' per reporting. Functional CB1 but perhaps best as a CB2.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality starter but overpaid at $24.3M cap hit. Dead cap of $26.1M makes a trade essentially impossible in 2026 — you'd accelerate more dead money than you'd save. Restructure is more likely. Only entering year 2 of 3.

players.availability_reasoning: Dead cap of $26.1M makes a 2026 trade financially destructive. A restructure could lower the cap hit. Giants need him as CB1 since Flott left and Newsome is on a prove-it deal. Available only in the sense that a post-June 1, 2027 move might make sense if he declines further.
players.injury_notes: Season-ending injury in 2024 with Saints (his final year in New Orleans). Played through the 2025 season mostly healthy.

players.name: Greg Newsome II
players.performance_evidence: Former 1st-round pick who had strong first 3 years in Cleveland then declined in 2024, was traded to Jaguars. 207 career tackles, 4 INTs, 43 PD in 71 games. 2023 was best year: 14 PD, 2 INTs with Browns. 1-year prove-it deal suggests uncertainty. Athletic CB (4.39 40, 40" vert).

trade_value.reasoning: Former 1st-round pick on a 1-year deal with only $3M guaranteed. Still just 25 with upside. If he regains 2023 form, he's a quality starting CB. Worth a late-round pick at the trade deadline if he plays well.

players.availability_reasoning: On a 1-year prove-it deal. Giants signed him as a stopgap after losing Cor'Dale Flott. If they draft a CB high, Newsome could become trade bait at the deadline. Low dead cap ($3M) means easy to move.
players.trade_buzz: Beat reporters note the 1-year deal 'all but screams what the actual plan is' — this is a placeholder while the Giants address CB in the 2026 draft.

players.name: Dru Phillips
players.performance_evidence: Led Giants in passes defensed (12) and tied for team lead with 2 INTs in 2025. Had 11 tackles in Week 2, 9 tackles in Week 7. 56-yard INT return vs Chargers in Week 4. Emerged as one of the defense's bright spots despite overall team struggles. Physical slot corner.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old ascending nickel corner on a cheap rookie deal with 2 years left. Nickel is an increasingly valuable position in today's NFL. His breakout 2025 season and low cost make him a high-value asset. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd.

players.availability_reasoning: Ascending young player the Giants view as a long-term building block in the secondary alongside Adebo and Nubin. Would take a significant overpay (2nd-round pick) to pry him loose. Not being shopped.
players.injury_notes: Calf injury in Week 9 limited him briefly. Played through most of the season.

players.name: Tyler Nubin
players.performance_evidence: Named 1st-team All-Rookie at safety by The Athletic's Dane Brugler in 2024. 76.0 PFF run-defense grade, 79.4 PFF tackling grade as rookie. Led all rookies in tackles before Week 13 foot injury in 2024. Full 2025 season — 811 snaps, 57.2 PFF overall grade (coverage was the weak area). Starting SS who is still developing.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old starting safety on a cheap rookie deal with 2 years left. Strong run defender and tackler. Coverage needs improvement but he's ascending. Safety position depresses value somewhat, but the combination of age + cost + starting production puts him in early-3rd range.

players.availability_reasoning: Young building block on the defense. Giants invested a 2nd-round pick in him and he's the starting SS next to Holland. Would need a clear overpay to consider moving him.
players.injury_notes: Season-ending foot injury in Week 13 of 2024 rookie year. Played full 2025 season.

players.name: Jevon Holland
players.performance_evidence: 301 career tackles, 5 sacks, 5 INTs, 5 forced fumbles in 4 years with Miami. PFWA All-Rookie Team in 2021. Versatile safety who can play single-high, in the box, or blitz. Had 96 tackles in 2022. Production dipped in later Miami years but has the talent to be a top-15 safety.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old versatile safety on a big contract ($17.5M cap hit) with dead cap of $20.2M making a 2026 trade financially destructive. Has a potential out after 2026. If he plays well, he's worth a late 2nd; if not, the Giants may move on after this season.

players.availability_reasoning: Dead cap of $20.2M makes a pre-season trade nearly impossible. However, there's a potential out after 2026 per Spotrac. If Holland underperforms, the Giants could move on after one year. Not being shopped but the financial structure gives flexibility after 2026.
players.trade_buzz: Some fan/media criticism that Holland was an overpay for a non-contender. His contract is restructurable per beat reporting.

players.name: Jason Sanders
players.performance_evidence: 6x Pro Bowler and 3x consecutive All-Pro (including 1st-team All-Pro in 2025) with the Dolphins. 116 career regular-season games. Ranks 3rd in Dolphins franchise history with 826 points. One of the NFL's premier kickers.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite kicker but K is the least valuable trade position. On a 1-year deal with no dead cap. Could fetch a conditional late-round pick from a team with kicker issues.

players.availability_reasoning: 1-year deal, no dead cap. If Giants struggle and become sellers at the deadline, an elite kicker could fetch a late-round pick from a contender. But unlikely to be actively shopped given the upgrade he represents over Graham Gano.
